root_cause.summary: nodejs/node parallel test `test-debugger-exec` (test/parallel/test-debugger-exec.js) failed because its `waitForInitialBreak` helper timed out waiting for the inspector debugger to emit a `break in` initial-break message. The helper, defined at `test/common/debugger.js:67-92`, awaits a regex `/break (?:on start )?in/i` against the debugger's stdout via `waitFor`. After 15 seconds without a match it throws `Error: Timeout (15000) while waiting for /break (?:on start )?in/i` from `test/common/debugger.js:92`. The captured `output` shows the debugger CLI did spin up successfully — the inspector printed `Debugger listening on ws://127.0.0.1:49508/...` and `Debugger attached.` and reached the `debug>` prompt — but the synchronous initial break that the test expects to see (the debugger should auto-pause on script start and print `break in <file>:1` in the CLI client) never appeared in the test's window. The single-test failure causes the runner to print `=== 1 tests failed` and `make: *** [test-ci] Error 1`, which propagates to GHA as `##[error]Process completed with exit code 2`. This is a macOS-arm64 runner timing flake against the debugger's break-emission timing, not a logic bug — the only signal the log gives is the timeout itself plus the partial debugger output where the connect/attach succeeded but break-in did not.

required_signals.value: Error: Timeout (15000) while waiting for /break (?:on start )?in/i

evidence_spans.reason: Test boundary marker: `=== release test-debugger-exec ===` + `Path: parallel/test-debugger-exec` + the `##[error]--- stderr ---` GHA annotation that opens the failure block. Identifies the exact failing test path.

Core failure block: source line `test/common/debugger.js:92` (the throw site), the literal `Error: Timeout (15000) while waiting for /break (?:on start )?in/i`, and the JS stack trace ending at the test entry `test-debugger-exec.js:73:1`. Self-contained — names file:line at all 4 stack frames, the timeout duration, the regex pattern that didn't match, and the call hierarchy from `waitInitialBreak` through `waitForInitialBreak` and `waitFor`.

Captured debugger output object showing the test DID receive `Debugger listening on ws://127.0.0.1:49508/...`, `connecting to 127.0.0.1:49508 ... ok`, `Debugger attached.`, and the `debug>` prompt — proving the connect/attach phase succeeded and the timeout was specifically on the break-in pattern, not on connection failure. Critical for distinguishing per-test timeout from connection/setup failure.
The reproduction `Command: out/Release/node /Users/runner/work/node/node/node/test/parallel/test-debugger-exec.js`. Lets a triager run the failing test in isolation.

Test runner summary block: `=== 1 tests failed`, `Failed tests: out/Release/node ...test-debugger-exec.js`, `make: *** [test-ci] Error 1`, and `##[error]Process completed with exit code 2.`. Confirms scope (exactly 1 test failed out of 5175) and exit-code propagation.

Test-suite header `Running 5175 tests` (one line up from the start of the dotted progress strip). Establishes that this is a single-test failure embedded in a 5175-test suite, useful for the diagnoser to estimate flake/scope.
